issues.title: AC-3 uses incorrect error-format terminology: "HTTP 422" does not apply to MCP tool responses
issues.description: AC-3 specifies: "returning HTTP 422 with an error code of INVALID_TRANSITION". MCP tools communicate via the Model Context Protocol, not HTTP. A tool call that fails returns a CallToolResult with isError=true and a structured content payload — there is no HTTP status code at the tool level. The correct specification should read: "returns a structured MCP error response with code INVALID_TRANSITION and a message identifying the illegal from→to pair". The intended behavior (reject invalid transitions with a machine-readable error code) is correct — only the error-format terminology is wrong. Fix required before AC-3 can be marked pass. Workaround: implementation team should interpret "HTTP 422" as "structured MCP error with equivalent semantics".

issues.title: AC-2: "HTTP 404" wrong for MCP context — must be structured MCP error with CRITERION_NOT_FOUND code
issues.description: AC-2 specifies verify_ac returns "HTTP 404" for a non-existent item_number. MCP tools do not return HTTP status codes. Must be: "returns a structured MCP error response with code CRITERION_NOT_FOUND". Same class of error as ISSUE-P049-1. Design should document the full error code table for all verify_ac failure modes (CRITERION_NOT_FOUND, INVALID_STATUS_TRANSITION, etc.).

issues.title: AC-3: "critical-tier" criteria undefined — no tier/is_critical field in proposal_acceptance_criteria schema
issues.description: AC-3 blocks prop_transition when "at least one criterion marked as critical-tier" fails. The proposal_acceptance_criteria schema (item_number, criterion_text, status, verified_by, verification_notes, verified_at) has no tier or is_critical field. Critical-tier criteria cannot be distinguished from standard criteria. Design must add: (1) an is_critical boolean or tier enum field to proposal_acceptance_criteria, (2) update add_acceptance_criteria to accept a critical flag per criterion, (3) update list_ac to return the tier. This is a blocking schema gap — the requires_ac='critical' gate mode cannot be implemented without it.

issues.title: AC-6 vs AC-7 design tension: idempotent overwrite destroys criterion status history needed by AC-7
issues.description: AC-6 specifies verify_ac "overwrites the previous verification record". AC-7 requires "the prior fail record is preserved in the audit trail". A single-row overwrite destroys the prior status unless a separate history mechanism exists. Resolution options: (a) criterion_status_history table logging each status transition, or (b) rely on proposal_version ledger capturing the full row delta on each verify_ac call (P053 dependency). Option (b) is preferred as it reuses the existing audit infrastructure. Design must explicitly document which mechanism is used and confirm P053 (Proposal Storage/Audit) as a dependency of P052.

issues.title: AC-6: "HTTP 422" wrong for MCP context + "BUILDING" state name may not match Standard RFC workflow definition
issues.description: AC-6 has two issues: (1) "HTTP 422" — MCP tools return structured errors (CallToolResult isError=true + SEQUENCE_BLOCKED code), not HTTP status codes. Same class as ISSUE-P049-1 and ISSUE-P052-1. (2) "BUILDING" — the observed workflow state from get_valid_transitions is DEVELOP, not BUILDING. If Standard RFC workflow defines stages with different names, AC-6 references the wrong state name. Design must: (a) document the canonical state names of the Standard RFC workflow, (b) update AC-6 to use the correct state name, (c) replace HTTP 422 with structured MCP error. Note: AC-9 (new) provides a corrected version of the sequence enforcement contract.

issues.title: P209: Execution blocker - 7th consecutive gate failure with zero implementation
issues.description: P209 has failed 7 consecutive gate reviews with zero implementation. All 8 ACs unmet. Design path mismatch (src/proposal-engine/ vs src/infra/). No implementation agent has touched codebase. Recommend reassigning or decomposing proposal.
